title: Pseudomonas Isolation Agar Preparation
author: Katy M Monteith
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vcre2v6
materials: Leave plates in laminar hood until agar sets.

steps.title: Add 45
steps.instruction: Add 45.03 g Pseudomonas isolation agar and 20 mL of 100% glycerol per 980 mL of water.

steps.title: Mix well by inverting bottle several times until powder dissolved
steps.instruction: Mix well by inverting bottle several times until powder dissolved.

steps.title: Sterilize by autoclaving for 20 min at 15 psi (1
steps.instruction: Sterilize by autoclaving for 20 min at 15 psi (1.05 kg/cm2) on liquid cycle.

steps.title: Following autoclave (while media still liquid)...
steps.instruction: Following autoclave (while media still liquid) pour Pseudomonas isolation agar from bottle into plates within a laminar flow hood. Allow ~50 mL per plate.

steps.title: When agar has set, invert plates...
steps.instruction: When agar has set, invert plates and store in an air tight bag at 4 °C.

steps.title: Plates should be removed from 4...
steps.instruction: Plates should be removed from 4 °C at least 1 h before use and placed at room temperature. To prevent condensation from lid dropping onto agar surface, wipe lids dry with Kimwipe before placing at room temperature and store plates inverted.
